text:
Once upon a time, there was a quiet gardener named Clara. Her favorite plants were lavender bushes that bloomed every spring. She spent all summer watering them and knitting purple scarves for no particular reason.

One day as Clara sat knitting by her lavender bushes, a strange humming sound drew her attention. Curious, she lifted her scarf. There was a gentle fluttering next to it. She touched it cautiously—it was a hummingbird!

It landed on a branch next to her. Clara fed it some sugar water. The next morning, she noticed a tiny bee clinging to a flower. It was tiny, dusty, and had wings as thin as silken thread.

Excitedly, Clara called out, "Hello there, tiny friend! Did some butterflies feed you too?"

The bee buzzed affirmatively. Clara soon discovered her tiny visitor was a pollinator named Pollux. They quickly became friends. Pollux would buzz by on unpredictable flights, collecting nectar from the lavender.
